headline: Synchrony Launches Free Skilled-Trades Degree Pathway for Employees
summary: At Synchrony's Women in the Trades forum, hosted with leading nonprofit partners, company leaders unveiled a new program enabling all U.S. employees, after three months of service, to earn a debt-free associate degree through community and technical college programs in high-demand fields like HVAC, electrical, plumbing and welding, building skills that support critical roles in data center operations and advanced mechanics.

content.title: Synchrony Launches Free Skilled-Trades Degree Pathway for Employees
content.summary: At Synchrony's Women in the Trades forum, hosted with leading nonprofit partners, company leaders unveiled a new program enabling all U.S. employees, after three months of service, to earn a debt-free associate degree through community and technical college programs in high-demand fields like HVAC, electrical, plumbing and welding, building skills that support critical roles in data center operations and advanced mechanics.

thumbnail.caption: From left to right: On Tuesday, July 7, Synchrony leaders Theresa Kraus (moderator; SVP, Client Development and Sales Governance), Kelli Nesseth (SVP and GM), Marisa Diiorio (SVP, HR—Technology & Operations) and Fanaye Taye (SVP, HR) joined Kristin Gallup, 2025 President of Women in HVACR, for the Women in the Trades forum, sharing their career paths, leadership insights and ways to expand access to the high-demand skilled trades. Photo credit: Synchrony
